operation: On the XY workplane, a 2D sketch is created containing an 8x6 rectangle. All four vertices of this rectangle are selected and modified with a 1.5-unit chamfer. After finalizing the sketch, the resulting octagonal profile is extruded 3 units to create a solid 3D body.
geometry: The model is an octagonal prism with a uniform cross-section. It takes the form of a thick rectangular block where the vertical corners have been replaced by flat diagonal faces, resulting in a symmetrical eight-sided footprint.
function: This component is designed as a simple structural block or spacer. The chamfered corners make it safer to handle and easier to fit into enclosures where corner clearance might be required.
